steps.title: Refute with praise
steps.content: The student wants to refute the claim that slow TV is boring and must use a quotation. The best sentence should directly push back against the "boring" label while quoting Heller's praise of the genre.

steps.title: Require both parts
steps.content: A uses the quotation but does not explicitly challenge the boring claim. B presents both sides without refuting the claim. D repeats the criticism instead of opposing it. The effective choice says slow TV is far from boring and supports that with Heller's quotation. The answer is C.
